premises.statement: Fluorine chemistry is excluded from this educational aqueous displacement family because F2 is exceptionally hazardous and requires a distinct reviewed model; no supported case may silently infer it.

Equal or less-reactive halogens do not displace the compared halide under this bounded aqueous activity series; a first-class no-reaction presentation is outside the current reaction-frame feature set.

Hydrofluoric acid is a weak acid that only partially dissociates in water, which is a different equilibrium reaction model from the full, one-step strong-acid dissociation this family represents; representing partial-dissociation equilibria is outside the current structural domain.

Hydrofluoric acid is a weak acid that only partially dissociates in water, a different equilibrium reaction model from the full, one-step strong-acid dissociation this family represents; representing partial-dissociation equilibria is outside the current structural domain.

Silver fluoride is soluble in water rather than an insoluble precipitate, so the standard silver-halide precipitation outcome does not extend to X = F; representing soluble-salt exceptions to a precipitation family is outside the current structural domain.
ChemSpec explicitly bounds this educational halide parameter to F, Cl, Br, and I; At and Ts are outside the modeled domain and no chemistry is inferred for them.

The 118 candidate records use IUPAC element symbols, names, atomic numbers, and periods; optional group values and conventional block assignments are candidate interpretations covered by the host-selected AI review.
The hydrogen halide acid is compatible with the authored observation predicate disappears for this family outcome.
The hydrogen halide acid is compatible with the authored observation predicate disappears for this family outcome.
The selected alkali metal is compatible with the authored observation predicate disappears for this family outcome.
Carbon dioxide is compatible with the authored observation predicate evolves for this family outcome.
The less-reactive displaced elemental halogen is compatible with the authored observation predicate forms.
Hydrogen is compatible with the authored observation predicate evolves for this rule outcome.
The silver halide precipitate is compatible with the authored observation predicate forms for this family outcome.
Silver bromide forms a cream precipitate, compatible with the authored observation predicate colour for this member-specific outcome.
Silver chloride forms a white precipitate, compatible with the authored observation predicate colour for this member-specific outcome.
Silver iodide forms a yellow precipitate, compatible with the authored observation predicate colour for this member-specific outcome.
Water is compatible with the authored observation predicate forms for this family outcome.

Mixing an aqueous strong monoprotic hydrogen halide acid with an aqueous alkali-metal hydroxide has the reviewed representative outcome HX + MOH -> MX + H2O, a proton transfer from the acid to the hydroxide oxygen followed by ionic association of the conserved cation and halide.

Mixing an aqueous strong monoprotic hydrogen halide acid with an aqueous alkali-metal bicarbonate has the reviewed representative outcome HX + MHCO3 -> MX + H2O + CO2: the acid protonates the bicarbonate's charged oxygen, an intramolecular proton transfer and bond reorganization releases carbon dioxide and water, and the conserved cation and halide associate into the salt.

Mixing two equivalents of an aqueous strong monoprotic hydrogen halide acid with an aqueous alkali-metal carbonate has the representative outcome 2 HX + M2CO3 -> 2 MX + H2O + CO2: two proton transfers form carbonic acid, whose explanatory bond reorganization releases carbon dioxide and water.
Contact between Li, Na, or K metal and water has the reviewed representative outcome 2 M + 2 H2O -> 2 MOH + H2.
In aqueous solution, a more reactive halogen displaces a less reactive halide according to X2 + 2 NaY -> 2 NaX + Y2 for the bounded order Cl > Br > I.

Mixing aqueous silver nitrate with an aqueous sodium chloride, bromide, or iodide has the reviewed representative outcome AgNO3 + NaX -> AgX (precipitate) + NaNO3, a pure ionic re-association with no covalent bond or electron change.
ChemSpec represents the alkali-metal fragment as an M+ site core with one domain-owned delocalized valence electron for explanatory execution.

ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal bicarbonate as an ionic association of an M+ cation and a hydrogen carbonate anion: carbon double-bonded to one carbonyl oxygen, singly bonded to a protonated hydroxyl oxygen, and singly bonded to one charged oxygen, one canonical Lewis structure with total charge -1.

ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal carbonate as an ionic assembly of two M+ cations and a carbonate anion represented by one canonical Lewis structure: carbon double-bonded to one neutral oxygen and singly bonded to two O- sites, with total anion charge -2.
ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal halide as an ionic association of an M+ cation and a monatomic X- anion.
ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal hydroxide as an ionic association of M+ and covalently bonded OH- components.
ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal nitrate as an ionic association of an M+ cation and the same NO3- anion structure as silver nitrate.
ChemSpec represents carbon dioxide as neutral molecular CO2 with two C=O double bonds.
ChemSpec represents each supported elemental halogen as a neutral diatomic molecule X2 with one shared single covalent bond and three lone pairs on each atom.
ChemSpec represents hydrogen gas as neutral molecular H2 with one localized H-H single bond.
ChemSpec represents each supported hydrogen halide as a neutral molecular H-X single covalent bond.
ChemSpec represents each supported silver halide precipitate as an ionic association of an Ag+ cation and a monatomic X- anion.

ChemSpec represents silver nitrate as an ionic assembly of an Ag+ cation and a covalently bonded NO3- anion using one canonical Lewis structure: one N=O double bond and two N-O single bonds carrying the -1 formal charges.
ChemSpec represents water as neutral molecular H2O with two localized O-H single bonds.

The listed H, O, Li, Na, K, Cl, Br, F, and I tuples are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for monoprotic acid / alkali-metal hydroxide neutralization; transient states are not claims of physical mechanism.

The listed C, O, H, Li, Na, K, Cl, Br, F, and I tuples, including transient carbocation and odd-electron oxygen states, are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for these rewrites; they are not claims of physical intermediates or mechanism.
The listed Li, Na, K, Ca, H, and O tuples are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for this family and its unsupported probe; they are not a claim of physical mechanism.

The listed Na, F, Cl, Br, and I tuples are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for the two-electron rewrite, expressed as two independently checked one-electron transfers; those steps are not a claim of physical mechanism.
The listed Ag, N, O, Na, Cl, Br, and I tuples are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for silver-halide precipitation; they are not a claim of physical mechanism.
